keyPoints: A copy constructor in C++ is a special constructor that creates a copy of an existing object.
It is used to create a new object from an existing object and copies all the member variables of the existing object to the new object.
The syntax of a copy constructor is :
Note: You can have multiple constructors, but the compiler will only call the one that matches the argument list.
